page: Amazon Web Services r8i.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 96 virtual CPUs mapped to 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations.

The instance is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local NVMe storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached options. Network connectivity is managed by the AWS Nitro hypervisor, delivering a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included.

In benchmark evaluations, the r8i.24xlarge demonstrates top-tier performance in multi-core processing, memory bandwidth for both small and large block read-only operations, and database operations. It achieves 171.48 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks. However, single-core CPU performance and PassMark memory scores fall into the average category. The presence of Intel AMX enables top-tier prompt processing speeds for large language models, though text generation speeds for large models remain average.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high density of DDR5 memory relative to the compute cores provides a specialized resource balance for memory-intensive workloads without the overhead of GPU hardware. It is designed for large-scale database operations, in-memory caching, software compilation, and LLM prompt processing workloads that benefit from high memory bandwidth and dedicated CPU allocation.
description: Amazon Web Services r8i.24xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 96 vCPUs, 48 physical cores, and a 3.9 GHz clock speed. It features 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 7 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, but lacks local storage. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it provides a 40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data shows top-tier performance in memory bandwidth, multi-core Geekbench scores, and Redis SET operations, alongside strong multi-threaded decompression. However, single-core CPU performance and PassMark memory scores are average. The instance supports advanced instruction sets like Intel AMX, which drives top-tier prompt processing speeds for large language models. This hardware profile is designed for memory-intensive databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise-scale software compilation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8i.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 3.9 GHz CPU, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8i.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 platform featuring 768 GB of DDR5 RAM and 96 vCPUs for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor.
768.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.
Top-tier memory bandwidth and Redis SET performance.
Intel AMX support delivering top-tier LLM prompt processing.
40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the AWS Nitro hypervisor.
No local storage included, requiring network-attached storage solutions.
